titles.title: COVID Fraud Transparency Act of 2025
COVID Fraud Transparency Act of 2026
COVID Fraud Transparency Act of 2026
To require the Inspector General of the Small Business Administration to submit a quarterly report on fraud relating to certain COVID-19 loans.
To require the Inspector General of the Small Business Administration to submit a quarterly report on fraud relating to certain COVID–19 loans.
COVID Fraud Transparency Act of 2026

action.text: Referred to the House Committee on Small Business.
Committee Consideration and Mark-up Session Held
Ordered to be Reported in the Nature of a Substitute by the Yeas and Nays: 23 - 0.
Reported (Amended) by the Committee on Small Business. H. Rept. 119-677.

calendar.text: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 590.

action.text: Mr. Williams (TX) moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended.
Considered under suspension of the rules.
DEBATE - The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 826.

vote.text: On motion to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended Agreed to by voice vote.

action.text: Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Small Business and Entrepreneurship.

committee.name: House Small Business
committee.activity: Reported By, Markup By, Referred To

bill.summary:
COVID Fraud Transparency Act of 2026
This bill requires the Small Business Administration's Office of Inspector General to report quarterly to Congress about fraud cases involving certain COVID-19 loans (e.g., Paycheck Protection Program loans).
The report must include the
number and total dollar amount of such loans,number of new cases of fraud and suspected fraud,number of fraud cases resolved, andtypes of such cases of fraud.The reporting requirements terminate two years after this bill is enacted.
